questions.question: Which tree is the largest of all in the poem? / கவிதையில் மிகப்பெரிய மரம் எது?
questions.explanation: The Banyan is described as the largest tree in the poem.

questions.question: What does the Peepul do in the breeze? / அரச மரம் காற்றில் என்ன செய்கிறது?
questions.explanation: The Peepul tree quivers (shakes quickly) in the breeze.

questions.question: The Tamarind tree provides... / புளிய மரம் எதைத் தருகிறது?
questions.explanation: The Tamarind tree gives us pleasant shade.

questions.question: Whose leaves are sharp as a blade? / யாருடைய இலைகள் கத்தி போல கூர்மையானவை?
questions.explanation: The leaves of the date palm are as sharp as a blade.

questions.question: What does the Teak tree give us? / தேக்கு மரம் நமக்கு என்ன தருகிறது?
questions.explanation: The Teak tree provides useful wood for building and furniture.

questions.question: Who is the author of the story 'When the Trees Walked'?
questions.explanation: Ruskin Bond is the author of the story 'When the Trees Walked'.

questions.question: Grandfather served in the Indian _____ Service.
questions.explanation: He served in the Indian Forest Service.

questions.question: Which tree is a 'show-off'? / பந்தா செய்யும் மரம் எது?
questions.explanation: The Peepul tree is nicknamed a 'show-off' because its leaves spin.

questions.question: The island was a small green _____. / அந்த தீவு ஒரு சிறிய பச்சை ____.
questions.explanation: The rocky island transformed into a green paradise with trees.

questions.question: Grandfather planted trees for _____ and _____.
questions.explanation: He believed trees are essential for animals and birds' food and shelter.

questions.question: Which tree moves with aerial roots? / விழுதுகளால் நகரும் மரம் எது?
questions.explanation: The banyan's aerial roots grow into the ground, letting it 'move' or spread.

questions.question: The author went to _____ for several years.
questions.explanation: Ruskin Bond went to England (and school) for many years after the war.

questions.question: A sentence that ends with '!' is called... / '!' குறியில் முடியும் வாக்கியம் எது?
questions.explanation: Exclamatory sentences express strong emotion and end with an exclamation mark.

questions.question: Choose: _____ luggage did you bring?
questions.explanation: Luggage is uncountable, so we use 'how much'.

questions.question: Choose: _____ colour do you want - red or green?
questions.explanation: When choosing from fixed options (red/green), we use 'which'.

questions.question: The opposite of 'Rough' is... / 'Rough' என்பதன் எதிர்ச்சொல் என்ன?
questions.explanation: Smooth is the direct antonym for rough.

questions.question: Ruskin Bond won the Padma Bhushan in ____.
questions.explanation: He received the Padma Bhushan award in 2014.

questions.question: Identify the rhyming pair: / எது எது எதுகையுள்ள சொற்கள்?
questions.explanation: Wood and Good share the same ending sound.

questions.question: The word 'Quiver' means... / 'Quiver' என்பதன் பொருள் என்ன?
questions.explanation: To quiver means to shake or tremble rapidly.

questions.question: Why was the author sent to boarding school? / ஆசிரியர் ஏன் உறைவிடப் பள்ளிக்கு அனுப்பப்பட்டார்?
options: b) Because of Second World War
questions.answer: b) Because of Second World War
questions.explanation: The Second World War forced him to attend a boarding school.

questions.question: Which tree sprouts on the window-sill in the rains?
questions.explanation: The mango seed sprouted on the window-sill during the monsoons.

questions.question: Grandmother preferred growing _____. / பாட்டி எதை வளர்க்க விரும்பினார்?
questions.explanation: Grandmother was mainly interested in her flower garden.

questions.question: What attracted butterflies? / பட்டாம்பூச்சிகளை ஈர்த்தது எது?
questions.explanation: Grandfather grew flowers specifically to attract butterflies.

questions.question: A time will come when the world will be one great _____. / உலகம் ஒரு பெரிய ____ ஆகும் காலம் வரும்.
questions.explanation: Grandfather warned that without trees, the earth would become a desert.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Woodman, spare that tree!'?
questions.explanation: George Morris is the poet who wrote that line.

questions.question: The island was located in a dry _____. / அந்த தீவு ஒரு காய்ந்த ____ ல் இருந்தது.
questions.explanation: They found a small island in the bed of a seasonal river.

questions.question: Plants and trees grow tall in the _____ Valley.
questions.explanation: The Doon Valley is described as having trees that grow very strong.

questions.question: The synonym of 'Beckoned' is... / 'Beckoned' என்பதன் பொருள்?
questions.explanation: Beckoned means to gesture to someone to come closer.

questions.question: Tamil Nadu has nearly _____ species of flora.
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has over 5000 species and represents a significant portion of India's flora.

questions.question: The author returned to Dehradun in the month of ____.
questions.explanation: The story mentions he returned in February.
